level2: others(Biomedical schema matching and data harmonization)

viewsInfo.description: Left-side control area for configuring matching parameters, managing candidate decisions, and revisiting prior curation actions.

quotes: The control panel ... serves two primary functions: configuration of matching parameters and keeping track of user actions
a shortcut panel for managing matching candidates
evidence.reasoning: The caption and UI-components section describe one grouped control area with three labeled regions.

subViews.description: Top-left toolbar for quick curation actions such as accepting or rejecting candidates, undoing or redoing edits, and importing or exporting data.

quotes: shortcut panel for managing matching candidates, undo/redo, importing datasets, and exporting results as CSV or JSON
undo/redo buttons

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Compact toolbar containing quick decision and session-management controls for schema curation.

quotes: shortcut panel for managing matching candidates

capabilities.description: Lets users commit or dismiss highlighted schema-match candidates from the quick-action toolbar.

quotes: accept, or reject candidates
managing matching candidates

capabilities.description: Supports undo and redo navigation plus dataset import and result export actions.

quotes: undo/redo buttons
importing datasets, and exporting results as CSV or JSON

subViews.description: Configuration form for source-attribute selection, filtering thresholds, matcher weighting, and related parameters that narrow the candidate set shown elsewhere.

quotes: control panel for filtering candidates
Users can select scoring algorithms, threshold values, and weighting factors

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Form-like control region used to configure matching parameters, thresholds, and filtering criteria before or during candidate exploration.

quotes: Users can configure the datasets/schemas, matcher parameters as well as modify matching thresholds

capabilities.description: Allows users to change scoring algorithms, threshold values, and matcher weighting factors that influence automated matching.

quotes: Users can select scoring algorithms, threshold values, and weighting factors for different similarity metrics

capabilities.description: Lets users narrow the visible match candidates to relevant subsets instead of reviewing all possible matches at once.

quotes: Users can filter matches by confidence thresholds
control panel for filtering candidates

subViews.description: Lower-left provenance display showing curation events in chronological order and enabling navigation back to prior decision points.

quotes: timeline graph showing the history of user actions
The provenance component records user interactions chronologically

y.description: Positions user-action events along a temporal sequence.

color.description: Uses icon or color variation to distinguish event types such as parameter change, accept, or reject.

y.description: Connects action nodes in chronological order.

quotes: Each interaction is represented as a discrete event on the timeline
icons indicating the type of action performed

capabilities.description: Shows a chronological record of parameter adjustments and accept or reject decisions made during curation.

quotes: history of user actions
records user interactions chronologically

capabilities.description: Lets users jump back to specific decision points in the curation provenance trail.

quotes: the ability to directly navigate to specific decision points

viewsInfo.description: Central coordinated analysis area that summarizes candidate matches, exposes schema structure, and provides multiple detail views for inspecting source-target relationships.

quotes: The Interactive Heatmap Panel ... provides a summary of the matches
bottom: an UpSet plot, value comparisons, and parallel coordinates
evidence.reasoning: The paper treats the central region as panel 2 with multiple coordinated internal regions, so the draft keeps one top-level view with multiple sub-views.

subViews.description: Matrix-based overview of match candidates with source attributes on rows, target attributes on the x-axis, and color intensity showing similarity scores.

quotes: matrix-based layout where color intensity encodes similarity scores
interactive heatmap panel displaying matching candidates with source attributes on the y-axis

x.description: Places target attributes across columns using the schema layout.

y.description: Places source attributes down the rows.

color.description: Uses color intensity to encode normalized similarity strength.
Status colors distinguish accepted, rejected, or highlighted cells.

quotes: matrix-based layout where color intensity encodes similarity scores
selected matches (cells)

processingSteps.name: Ensemble schema matching score aggregation
processingSteps.description: Combines multiple schema-matching methods into a weighted candidate score that is summarized in the heatmap for interactive exploration.

quotes: combines multiple matching methods
The matching score displayed in the heatmap is computed as the average of an ensemble of matchers

capabilities.description: Lets users scan many source-target candidates at once to identify promising or problematic matches.

quotes: summarize matches across large schemas
provides a summary of the matches

capabilities.description: Supports clicking or hovering a match cell to open detailed evidence for a specific source-target attribute pair.

quotes: By selecting a cells, users can examine a specific attribute pair in detail
When a user hovers over a cell

subViews.description: Detail-on-demand pair of histograms that compares the source and target attribute value distributions for a selected candidate match.

quotes: display side-by-side histograms with source and target value distributions
Expanded view of the value distribution histograms

x.description: Organizes the source attribute's value bins or categories along the horizontal axis.

y.description: Encodes the count or prevalence for each source value bin.

x.description: Organizes the target attribute's value bins or categories along the horizontal axis.

y.description: Encodes the count or prevalence for each target value bin.

quotes: side-by-side histograms
source (2A) and target (2B) attributes

capabilities.description: Shows whether source and target attributes have compatible value distributions beyond their similarity score.

quotes: verify semantic alignment by inspecting the actual data values

subViews.description: Hierarchical x-axis display that groups target attributes into supercategories, categories, and individual columns with connectors showing their relationships.

quotes: The x-axis of the heatmap displays a hierarchical organization of target attributes
curved connectors represent hierarchical relationships

x.description: Places schema categories and columns along the horizontal hierarchy.

color.description: Uses color to distinguish categories and supercategories.

source.description: Starts a connector from a parent category or supercategory.

target.description: Ends a connector at a child category or column.

quotes: three-level hierarchy
curved connectors represent hierarchical relationships

capabilities.description: Provides structural context about how target attributes are grouped inside the biomedical schema.

quotes: providing a concise visualization of relationships present in complex biomedical schemas

capabilities.description: Lets users hover categories or columns to emphasize selected hierarchy levels and their relationships.

quotes: Our interactive hover functionality allows users to highlight specific levels

subViews.description: Tabular-plot hybrid view that reveals which matchers support a candidate, their weights, and the resulting weighted average score.

quotes: UpSet plot visualization
matcher weights
the matchers that produced a given candidate

x.description: Places candidate combinations or matcher entries across the UpSet layout.

y.description: Places contributing matchers on separate rows.

color.description: Uses the shared heatmap palette to indicate the combined score strength.

x.description: Connects dots belonging to the same candidate recommendation.
Encodes current weight assigned to each matcher.

y.description: Associates each bar with a matcher.

quotes: each matcher's recommendation is encoded by a dot
matcher weights

capabilities.description: Shows which matchers recommended a candidate and how strongly they contribute to the combined score.

quotes: identify which matchers support a particular match
weighted average score for each candidate

subViews.description: Tabbed table-like view that aligns unique source values with corresponding target-column values for the selected candidate match.

quotes: value comparisons
one-to-one mapping between the unique values from the source column and the corresponding values from the candidate target column

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Table-like comparison view that lists source values beside matched target values for the currently selected attribute pair.

quotes: presents a one-to-one mapping between the unique values from the source column and the corresponding values

capabilities.description: Displays concrete value mappings so users can inspect how source and target attribute values align.

quotes: the value comparison for these attributes is shown in Fig. 3 (2B)

subViews.description: Named tab or mode for source-target relationship inspection within panel 2B, but the available evidence does not support a defensible visual-style reconstruction.

quotes: parallel coordinates for understanding the source-target relationships

capabilities.description: Indicates an additional analysis mode for understanding source-target relationships, without committing to unsupported visual details.

quotes: parallel coordinates for understanding the source-target relationships

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Named analysis mode within the 2B tab set for understanding source-target relationships. The caption confirms its existence, but the reviewed evidence does not justify a stronger claim about its visible chart form.

quotes: parallel coordinates for understanding the source-target relationships

viewsInfo.description: Right-side contextual panel that explains the selected match candidate and shows metadata for the target attribute under consideration.

quotes: Agent panel to guide accept/reject decisions
agent explanation panel showing LLM validation results

subViews.description: Explanation cards describing why the LLM-based agent considers a selected source-target pair to be a match, including confidence scores and evidence categories.

quotes: explanations generated by the LLM-based agent
each explanation category is presented with a confidence score

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Stack of explanation cards and progress bars summarizing semantic, value-based, name-based, and historical evidence for the selected match.

quotes: presented with a confidence score visualized as a progress bar

processingSteps.name: LLM-based explanation generation
processingSteps.description: Generates validation explanations for selected schema matches by combining semantic, value, naming, and historical evidence.

processingSteps.name: Retrieval-augmented historical memory
processingSteps.description: Stores and retrieves validated matches, terminology relationships, and correction patterns so later explanations can align with prior user behavior and domain conventions.

quotes: LLM validation results with reasoning
The LLM agent learns from user interactions over time through a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) component

capabilities.description: Shows the rationale and confidence for a proposed match using several explanation categories.

quotes: LLM validation results with reasoning
helps users gauge the system's certainty

capabilities.description: Allows users to confirm or correct explanation quality so the agent can incorporate that feedback later.

quotes: Users can provide feedback on explanations

subViews.description: Structured metadata display for the selected target attribute, including node, category, type, description, and sometimes value-distribution context.

quotes: display of target attribute properties, including node name, category, type, descriptions, and value distributions
attribute definitions, data types, and value constraints

nonVisualViewSpec.description: Inspector-like metadata card for the currently selected target attribute.

quotes: display of target attribute properties

capabilities.description: Shows schema-level metadata and descriptive context for the target attribute involved in the selected candidate match.

quotes: the description of primary_diagnosis from GDC is shown
attribute definitions, data types, and value constraints
